title: [Feature]: Add tamper-evident chain-of-custody metadata
problem: High-assurance event custody workflows need to detect unexpected record changes after a message has been written to a durable destination. nats-sinks currently preserves message data and metadata, but it does not define a common tamper-evident record envelope with hashes, commit identifiers, or optional hash chaining.
proposal: Design and implement optional tamper-evident custody metadata that can record payload hash, normalized metadata hash, sink commit identifier, hash algorithm, hash key version when applicable, and an optional previous-record hash for append-only destinations. The feature should be destination-neutral and should allow sinks to expose what they can support without changing ACK ownership.
users: Security reviewers, auditors, mission-support operators, compliance teams, platform engineers, and future sink authors who need a consistent audit story across database and file destinations.
delivery_semantics: Custody metadata must be computed before sink write and returned or confirmed only after durable sink success when sink-specific commit identifiers are involved. Failure to compute or verify required custody metadata should fail closed and must not lead to ACK before durable success.
security: Hashing must not be described as encryption. Cryptographic algorithms must be allow-listed. Payload hashes can still reveal information about repeated payloads, so documentation must explain privacy trade-offs. Logs must not include full payloads, keys, or sensitive metadata while computing custody evidence.
acceptance: A custody metadata model is documented with supported algorithms and privacy notes.
Core helpers can compute stable hashes over normalized payload and metadata structures.
Oracle and file sink design notes explain how custody metadata would be stored.
Hash chaining is optional and documented as destination-dependent.
Tests cover deterministic hashing, redaction, malformed inputs, and no ACK on custody failure.
tests: Add unit tests for canonicalization, hash computation, algorithm allow-listing, malformed metadata, large input bounds, file sink output, Oracle row mapping, public API exports, configuration validation, and commit-then-ack ordering when custody metadata generation fails.
documentation: Update docs/tamper-evident-custody.md, docs/security.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/oracle-sink.md, docs/file-sink.md, docs/use-cases, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md with custody behavior, examples, non-goals, and verification guidance.
closeout: Close only after the release containing chain-of-custody support is published. Include release tag, algorithms, storage behavior, tests run, and follow-up work for signatures if separate.
